name: Riesentraube
image_alt: Large trusses of small pointed red cherry tomatoes on the vine
description: A prolific German heirloom cherry tomato — the name means 'giant bunch of grapes'. Produces enormous trusses of small, pointed red fruits with a sweet, rich, old-fashioned tomato flavor.
history: A pre-1850s German heirloom, one of the oldest documented cherry tomatoes in cultivation. Grown widely in Pennsylvania Dutch communities and nearly lost before being preserved by seed savers in the late 20th century.
seasonal_quirks: Clusters become so heavy they can snap vines — tie trusses to support as they develop. Flavor peaks in late summer heat. Very heat tolerant for a cherry tomato.
harvest_cues: Fruits turn deep red and develop a slight give. Pick entire trusses when most fruits on the cluster are ripe — they hold well on the truss for several days.
variety_notes: The pointed blossom end and very large cluster size distinguish it from other cherries. Flavor is notably richer than most modern cherry varieties.
preservation: Excellent slow-roasted and packed in olive oil. Also good for cherry tomato jam.
